mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) when UNRATE is below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1500k units for two consecutive months signals a severe housing contraction that depresses consumer wealth and economic activity, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1500 for two months, SPX 60-day returns are not negative in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with very low policy rates creates negative real interest rates that drive asset inflation and risk-taking, boosting equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When trigger condition holds, SPX 20-day returns are not positive (median return <= 0) over a 2-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is above its 12-month moving average, further increases in production indicate expanding output that lifts corporate profits and equity prices. When below the average, declines deepen recession fears.
failure_condition: INDPRO above 12-month MA fails to yield positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of months, or the rank IC of INDPRO changes is <0.05 during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A policy rate near zero signals deep economic weakness and deflation risk, causing equity investors to discount shrinking future cash flows at a higher risk premium, leading to negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, SPX 5-day returns are not negative on average (mean return >= 0) over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in industrial production provide an early signal of a broad economic contraction, which erodes corporate earnings and drives negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After three consecutive INDPRO declines, SPX 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 60% of episodes or the average return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth alongside contracting payrolls indicates consumption sustained by savings or credit rather than income, an unsustainable dynamic that portends a downturn and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 2% and PAYEMS 3-month average change < 0
failure_condition: When trigger condition holds, SPX 20-day returns are not negative on average (mean return >= 0) over any 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are persistently below 1500k units, the housing bust depresses residential investment and household wealth, producing negative spillovers to equity markets over a multi-month horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1500, SPX 60-day returns are not negative (median return >= 0) or the rank IC of HOUST level is not negative at the 10% significance level over rolling 3-year windows.
